participants.name: 사회복지학
participants.professional_persona: 사회복지학 박사 과정에 재학 중이며, BK21 교육연구단에서 지역사회 복지 격차 실태 분석 연구를 수행하고 있습니다. 연구 장학금과 별도로 프로젝트 참여 연구비를 받고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 양적·질적 연구 방법론, SPSS 및 NVivo 활용 데이터 분석, 욕구 조사 및 프로그램 평가 능력을 갖추고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 사회복지 정책 평가 전문가로 성장하여, 증거 기반의 복지 정책 수립에 기여하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 한양대학교 (og6bcj)
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교 전자공학부 교수로, BK21 미래인재양성사업 교육연구팀을 이끌고 있습니다. 반도체 회로설계 분야에서 석·박사과정생 15명을 지도하며 매월 연구장학금을 지급하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: VLSI 회로설계 및 저전력 반도체 설계 전문가로, 20편 이상의 SCIE 논문을 게재했습니다. 국가연구과제 수행 및 산학협력 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구팀의 중간평가에서 우수 등급을 받아 지원금을 확대하고, 팀 내 연구성과를 국제학술대회에 지속적으로 발표하는 것을 목표로 합니다.

participants.name: 의학
participants.professional_persona: 의학 계열 박사과정생으로, 뇌과학 연구에 매진하고 있습니다. BK21 프로그램을 통해 매월 130만 원의 장학금을 지원받으며, 임상과 기초 연구를 병행하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: fMRI 데이터 분석과 신경과학 실험 설계에 능숙하며, R과 MATLAB을 활용한 통계 분석이 가능합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 향후 의과대학 교수로서 후학을 양성하고, 치매 조기 진단 기술을 개발하는 연구를 주도하고 싶습니다.

participants.skills_and_expertise: 유기합성과 분광학 분석에 능하고, 논문 게재 실적이 2편 있습니다. 국제 학회 발표 경험도 있습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a master's student in mechanical engineering at Hanyang University, I see the BK21 program funding as a direct enabler of my research. The total budget for 2022 is stated as 408,080 million KRW, which I take as ground truth. For graduate scholarship slots, I estimate about 19,000 slots nationally based on the 12,600 future talent and 6,400 innovative talent participants per year, totaling 19,000. This aligns with my experience that most eligible students in my group receive stipends. For postdoc positions, I estimate around 1,200 nationally, considering the 577 education research groups and typical group sizes of 2-3 postdocs, but many groups have fewer due to budget constraints. My perspective as a student emphasizes the direct impact of funding on my stipend and research capacity.
evidence: The policy states the annual budget is 408,080 million KRW for education research group support, which I use as the total funding allocated.
Graduate student participation numbers of 12,600 (future talent) and 6,400 (innovative talent) sum to 19,000, matching typical slot counts per year.
With 577 education research groups and an average of 2 postdocs per group, the total would be ~1,154, but budget limits reduce this to about 1,200 nationally.
As a student, I know that stipend slots are prioritized for enrolled students, so the 19,000 figure reflects actual funded positions.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group reached strong consensus on all three input variables, treating the total funding allocated as a fixed policy parameter of 408,080 million KRW. Graduate scholarship slots were uniformly estimated at 19,000, derived by summing the stated 12,600 future talent and 6,400 innovative talent participants. Postdoc positions were consistently estimated at 1,200, based on 577 education research groups with typical allocations of 2-3 postdocs per group, tempered by budget constraints. No disagreements emerged; all participants anchored their reasoning directly on the policy document's stated figures and their own operational experience.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of an education research group in electronics engineering at Hanyang University, I see the activities phase as directly translating the fixed budget into operational actions. The total funds disbursed must match the allocated 408,080 million KRW, as the policy mandates two installments (70% in March, 30% in September) with no reason for delay given the program's established schedule. Evaluation panels are convened for three formal meetings: annual evaluation, mid-term evaluation, and compliance checks, as per the policy's evaluation cycle. Consulting sessions are offered to groups that request them; the policy states 280 out of 578 groups received consulting, which aligns with my experience that many groups seek guidance to improve performance. Compliance checks target the 205 innovative talent training groups, as specified in the policy for implementation verification. Budget adjustments based on evaluation outcomes are handled by the Project Oversight Committee, but at this phase, funds are disbursed as planned without reallocation yet.
evidence: The policy explicitly states the budget is paid in two installments: 70% in March (285,656 million KRW) and 30% in September (122,424 million KRW), totaling 408,080 million KRW, which I take as ground truth for disbursement.
Evaluation panels are convened for annual evaluation, mid-term evaluation, and compliance checks, as per the policy's evaluation schedule, leading to three formal meetings per year.
The policy document indicates that 280 out of 578 education research groups requested and received consulting, reflecting voluntary participation based on group needs.
Compliance checks are executed specifically for the 205 innovative talent training groups, as mandated by the policy for implementation verification in October-November.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral researcher in social welfare at Hanyang University, I see the outputs phase as the direct result of the funded activities. With 19,000 graduate scholarship slots and 1,200 postdoc positions funded, the research output per participant is key. Based on my own experience and observations in my research group, a typical graduate student produces about 1-2 publications per year, and postdocs about 2-3. Given that the program supports participants for the full year, I estimate an average of 2 publications per supported participant (graduate and postdoc combined), yielding about 38,000 publications. For international conference presentations, I estimate about 0.4 per participant, as many students present at least once during their degree, but not all do so annually. This gives about 7,600 presentations. These estimates assume high compliance with reporting, as 575 out of 578 groups submitted self-evaluation reports, indicating strong adherence to program requirements.
evidence: Based on my own research group, graduate students typically publish 1-2 papers per year, and postdocs 2-3, so an average of 2 per supported participant is reasonable.

International conference presentations are less frequent; in my experience, about 40% of participants present at an international conference annually, yielding 7,600 from 20,200 participants.
High compliance with self-evaluation reporting (575 out of 578 groups) suggests that research output data will be reliably captured and reported.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral researcher in social welfare at Hanyang University, I see the outcomes phase as the direct result of the funded activities and outputs. With 19,000 graduate students and 1,200 postdocs supported, and high compliance in reporting, the employment rate is influenced by network effects, training quality, and reputation. The policy targets a gradual increase from 86.7% baseline, but given the declining trend, I estimate 84.2% for 2022, reflecting my observation that many graduates in social welfare face job market challenges. International collaboration rate is boosted by conference support and collaboration incentives; I estimate 40% based on my experience that about 40% of participants present at international conferences annually. The full-time faculty lecture ratio is improved by the program's emphasis on quality mentoring; I estimate 75% based on my department's shift toward full-time faculty. Evaluation and consulting feedback loops help refine these outcomes by identifying weaknesses and promoting best practices.
evidence: The policy targets a 0.3%p annual increase from 86.7% baseline, but the declining trend suggests actual employment rate may be lower; my estimate of 84.2% reflects this and my personal observation of job market difficulties for social welfare graduates.

International collaboration rate is supported by conference funding and collaboration incentives; with 7,600 international conference presentations out of 20,200 participants, a 40% rate is consistent with my experience that many students present at least once during their degree.
Full-time faculty lecture ratio improves due to program design emphasizing quality mentoring; my department has increased full-time faculty involvement, leading to an estimated 75% ratio.

Evaluation and consulting feedback loops, such as the 280 consulting sessions and annual evaluations, help identify areas for improvement, contributing to gradual enhancement of employment and collaboration outcomes.
